preview_text:
The model forecasts Spain to win with 91% probability, leaving Cape Verde with a negligible 0.0% chance. This reflects a decisive asymmetry; the underdog's path to victory registers below the model's precision threshold, though draws remain plausible at nearly 9%.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Spain heavily favored across all outcomes. A Cape Verde victory appears structurally unlikely under the model; the competitive range narrows to Spain victory or stalemate. The 91–9 split between decisive and drawn outcomes suggests Spain is expected to control the match, though a draw emerges as the only realistic alternative result.

The model and sportsbooks diverge meaningfully on Cape Verde's chances. The market assigns the underdog 3.7%, roughly 4 percentage points above the model's floor. Polymarket pricing sits between the two: 3.3% for Cape Verde, 90% for Spain. On Spain's win probability, the three sources cluster tightly (88–91%), indicating broad agreement on the favorite. The dashboard offers no judgment on which source better reflects true upset risk.
